payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was not returned because it is superseded; superseded memories are retained for history/debugging but filtered from current recall results.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active, non-superseded memory matching Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered because it has been marked superseded and should not be presented as current.

entity_resolution.reason: The query refers to Sam associated with Goldman; recall resolved the active memory to mem_new while excluding the superseded predecessor.

payload.intent: Explain recall behavior for Sam/Goldman memories, specifically why mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the current memory for Sam from Goldman and was eligible for recall.
mem_old also matched the query context but was filtered because it is superseded by mem_new.

details: Explained why mem_old was filtered as superseded.
Did not present mem_old as a current fact.

repair_options: If mem_old should still be visible, inspect whether the superseded flag or superseded_by link was set incorrectly.
If mem_new is wrong, correct or delete mem_new and re-evaluate whether mem_old should be restored.
If both memories should coexist, change the relationship from supersession to distinct contextual facts.
